mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are uncorrelated with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0 percent threshold, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in 70 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and investment demand expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered months, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term equity returns as corporate margins expand.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent, or when INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate wage income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 24-month Rank IC falls below 0.02, or positive payroll surprises consistently precede negative 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation acceleration above 0.3 percent forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in 65 percent of months where inflation exceeds the threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power after 2005.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth combined with falling unemployment creates a synchronized macroeconomic expansion that boosts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals demand destruction and margin compression, which triggers negative short-term equity returns as investors anticipate earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of tightening periods with falling production, or the predictive relationship reverses when FEDFUNDS growth slows.
